64px + dict synth: new quick/medium/overnight configs, experiment sweep

Quick config: 64px height, miw=2, 100K limit, 3s cooldown (was 48px, miw=1)
- 64px + 3s cooldown fixed MPS thermal throttling (fwd 20ms→185ms at epoch 7)
- 64px is FASTER per epoch when not throttled (10s vs 14s for 48px)

Experiment results (64px, 15ep quick budget):
- miw=1 25K:     9.45% CER / 29.9% WER (new 64px baseline)
- miw=1 50K+dict: 8.59% CER / 28.5% WER (37K synthetic dict words added)
- miw=2 50K:     7.45% CER / 34.7% WER (image variety helps CER)
- miw=3 75K:     7.12% CER / 32.4% WER (best CER, 13/15 epochs)
- miw=2+dict 86K: 8.88% CER / 41.3% WER (9/15ep, cache rebuild cost)

Medium config: 64px, miw=4, 100K, 50ep, 3s cooldown
Overnight config: 64px, miw=0 (unlimited ~200K), 100ep, big arch (d=384, dec=4)

Also: generate 37,931 synthetic images for dictionary words not in training,
add synthetic_dict to ingest pipeline, update CVIT benchmark script for full run

commit.message:
feat: Phase 54C — Human review + externalization closure

Institution-grade human review layer for ambiguous evidence, controlled
banker actions with required rationale, borrower-safe feedback loop,
governed outbound orchestration, and borrower package alignment.

Migration:
- condition_evidence_reviews — 7-state review queue with reason categories,
  ambiguity flags, reviewer tracking, and borrower-safe explanations

Review engine (4 modules):
- evidence-review-types.ts — canonical review states + decision types
- queueEvidenceReview.ts — auto-queue ambiguous evidence
- applyEvidenceReviewDecision.ts — accept/partial/reject/clarify/waive
  with required fields (reject+clarify require borrower-safe explanation,
  waive requires internal rationale)
- deriveReviewQueueInsights.ts — stale items, repeated ambiguity, wait
  state indicators for banker visibility

Borrower feedback:
- explainReviewOutcomeForBorrower.ts — per-outcome borrower-safe
  explanations for reject, partial accept, clarification, waive

Outbound orchestration:
- deriveOutboundGuidanceActions.ts — event-driven, throttle-aware,
  draft-first for non-transactional, approval tracking

Externalization:
- buildBorrowerActionPackage.ts — coherent package from live guidance +
  review state, no contradictions between portal/package/outbound

API routes:
- GET /api/deals/[dealId]/review-queue — queue + insights (Clerk auth)
- POST /api/deals/[dealId]/review-queue/[reviewId] — decision actions
  (Clerk auth, field validation per action type)

CI guards (19 new tests, 96 total):
- Review queue contract (5): types, states, helpers exist
- Review action contract (4): required fields per action type
- Auth boundary (2): staff auth only, no borrower token
- Package/guidance alignment (4): builder exists, references live state
- Placeholder regression (1): no TODO/placeholder in review modules
- Outbound governance (3): throttle, draft-first, approval tracking

Add --diff-files and --diff-lines flags for git-based filtering (#56)

* Fix help text snapshots for --changed flag

The --changed flag added to SharedArgs in #55 changed the help output
for query, check, test, and run commands. Regenerate snapshots.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013pgjXrFTy1L85xQeqCaDm2

* Add --diff flag for hunk-level match filtering

Add a `--diff` CLI parameter and config field that restricts matches to
only those whose line ranges overlap with changed hunks in a git diff.
While `--changed` filters at the file level, `--diff` goes further by
filtering individual matches at the line level.

Architecture:
- New `ResultFilter` trait (filter.rs) with `include(&Match)` and
  `include_file()` methods, applied at the query engine level inside
  `query_files_multi()` and `run_rules()` so downstream code receives
  pre-filtered results
- `DiffHunkFilter` implementation (pipeline/git.rs) parses `git diff -U0`
  output to extract per-file hunk line ranges, then filters matches by
  overlap detection
- `--diff` on SharedArgs, `diff` field on all operation structs and config
  format (root-level and per-operation, same merge semantics as `changed`)

Example: `tractor check src/**/*.rs -x "//TODO" --diff "main..HEAD"`
only reports TODOs on lines that were actually changed vs main.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013pgjXrFTy1L85xQeqCaDm2

* Rename --changed/--diff to --diff-files/--diff-lines

Both flags take a git diff spec. The new names make their scope explicit:
- --diff-files: only process files that appear in the diff
- --diff-lines: only report matches overlapping changed hunks

Config keys use kebab-case (diff-files, diff-lines) via serde rename.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013pgjXrFTy1L85xQeqCaDm2

* Fix git tests for shallow clones (CI compatibility)

Use "HEAD HEAD" (empty diff) instead of "HEAD~1 HEAD" in the
git_changed_files_in_repo and git_diff_hunks_in_repo tests.
Shallow clones (actions/checkout with default fetch-depth: 1)
don't have HEAD~1 available.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013pgjXrFTy1L85xQeqCaDm2

* Replace shallow-clone-dependent git tests with self-contained repos

The git_changed_files_in_repo and git_diff_hunks_in_repo tests used
HEAD~1 on the host repo, which fails in CI shallow clones. Replace
with tests that init their own temp git repos with real commits and
diffs, verifying actual behavior:

- git_changed_files_detects_changed_file: two commits, checks both
  modified and added files appear in the changed set
- diff_hunk_filter_from_spec_with_real_repo: modifies specific lines,
  verifies matches on changed lines pass and unchanged lines don't
- git_changed_files_excludes_deleted_files: verifies --diff-filter=ACMR
  correctly excludes deleted files

https://claude.ai/code/session_013pgjXrFTy1L85xQeqCaDm2

* Show RANGE as value name for --diff-files/--diff-lines, run CI tests in release mode

- Add value_name = "RANGE" to both flags so help shows
  `--diff-files <RANGE>` instead of `--diff-files <DIFF_FILES>`
- Run unit tests with --release in CI so the release binary is already
  built when integration/snapshot tests need it, removing a redundant
  `cargo build --release` step

https://claude.ai/code/session_013pgjXrFTy1L85xQeqCaDm2

Deduplicate the 3-line preamble (cwd + build_filters + resolve_files)

commit.message:
feat: murmuration protocol + living docs + SolarPunk Lab

MURMUR.yml — Flock intelligence. Every engine writes a signal after
running. MURMUR reads all signals, computes flock energy (0-1),
identifies hot/cold engines, broadcasts consensus. No central
coordinator. Runs after every OMNIBRAIN + daily.

MUTATE.yml — Updated to read murmur_consensus.json. Parent selection
is now weighted toward hot engines instead of pure random. The mutation
lottery is still a lottery — but the flock tilts the odds.

LIVING_DOCS.yml — Applies murmuration to documentation. Triggers
whenever any data/ source updates. AMPLIFY.md gets current metrics +
top research citations (arXiv, Semantic Scholar) auto-injected.
press.html gets live academic backing block. lab/ README created.
Static files are now living surfaces.

lab/ — SolarPunk Lab directory. Experimental engines live here before
graduating to mycelium/. Engines graduate when: syntax passes, 3+
cycles without error, output usable by another engine.

commit.message:
fix(index): invalidate prop_index in WriteTx::commit — closes #259 (#266)

* perf: cache label_row_counts to eliminate per-label HWM disk reads (closes #186 closes #190)

Previously, every read query opened a fresh NodeStore (starting with an empty
HWM cache) and called hwm_for_label() for each registered label to build
label_row_counts.  With N labels this caused N syscalls (fs::read of hwm.bin)
before a single node was scanned.

Changes:
- Add label_row_counts: RwLock<HashMap<LabelId, usize>> to GraphDb::Inner;
  built once at open() and refreshed inside invalidate_catalog() (which is
  already called after every write commit).
- Add Engine::new_with_all_caches() that accepts an optional pre-built
  label_row_counts, short-circuiting the per-label HWM reads.  The existing
  Engine::new() and Engine::new_with_cached_index() delegate to it with
  None, preserving all existing call sites outside GraphDb.
- Add build_label_row_counts_from_disk() free function used by both open()
  and invalidate_catalog().
- Add GraphDb::build_read_engine() helper that wraps Engine::new_with_all_caches
  with all three caches (PropertyIndex, CSR map, label row counts).
- Update all read-query engine-construction paths to use build_read_engine().
- ReadTx::execute passes the cached row counts directly from DbInner.

Effect: Q1 point-lookup queries no longer pay O(n_labels) disk-read overhead
per execution.  The cost is moved once to the write path (inside the existing
invalidate_catalog() call) where it is negligible alongside WAL commit I/O.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(index): invalidate prop_index + rebuild label_row_counts in WriteTx::commit (closes #259)

When a caller used WriteTx directly (begin_write / merge_node / commit)
rather than going through GraphDb::execute, the shared property-index
cache was not cleared on commit.  Subsequent reads would clone the stale
cached index (which had the pre-write BTree), call build_for, hit a cache
hit (the key was already in `loaded`), and return wrong results for inline
prop filters like `{name: 'ClassA'}`.

Fix: add Step 12 to WriteTx::commit — clear prop_index after every commit.
Also rebuild label_row_counts from the freshly-opened catalog opened in
Step 11 (no extra I/O), so COUNT(*) fastpaths are also kept fresh.

GraphDb-level functions that call invalidate_prop_index() after commit
perform an extra harmless clear; the extra generation bump is safe.

Add four regression tests covering single WriteTx, two WriteTx without
prewarm, two WriteTx with prewarm, and the exact scenario from the
issue report (seeded label + two WriteTx operations).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(api): Cypher MATCH...DELETE r edge deletion (closes #219) (#260)

- Add Engine::scan_match_mutate_edges: scans CSR + delta for matching
  (src, dst, rel_type) tuples given a 2-node+1-hop MATCH pattern,
  supporting src/dst label filters and inline prop filters.
- Add is_edge_delete_mutation helper: detects when DELETE var refers
  to a relationship variable rather than a node variable.
- Route MATCH (a)-[r:REL]->(b) DELETE r to the edge-delete path in
  all three execute_match_mutate variants (with params, without params,
  and the batch-mutation path inside execute_write_batch).
- Fix EdgeStore::delete_edge to handle post-CHECKPOINT edges: if the
  edge is not in the delta log, fall back to rewriting the CSR forward
  and backward files with the target (src_slot, dst_slot) excised.
- Invalidate CSR map cache after edge deletion so subsequent MATCH
  queries see the updated graph.
- Add invalidate_csr_map() to GraphDb::delete_edge (public API).
- Add 3 Cypher-level acceptance tests in delete_edge.rs:
  cypher_match_delete_rel_removes_edge,
  cypher_match_delete_rel_with_src_prop_filter,
  cypher_match_delete_rel_after_checkpoint.
